title: Kaye Adams Says Stories About Her Are Not True
summary: Kaye Adams is a TV and radio presenter who used to work for BBC Scotland. She left her job in October after someone at the BBC complained about how she acted. Kaye says that stories about her being mean to an intern and using a bad word are not true. She feels very sad because she worked hard for 15 years, and now people are saying things that hurt her. Kaye posted on Instagram to tell everyone that these stories are false. The BBC says they do not talk about individual people, but they take complaints seriously. Kaye is also on other shows like Loose Women and was on Strictly Come Dancing. The BBC has a program called 'Call it Out' to help staff report bad behavior.

background_read: Kaye Adams is a well-known presenter in Scotland and the UK. She has worked for the BBC for many years, hosting radio shows and appearing on TV programs like Loose Women and Strictly Come Dancing. The BBC is a public broadcaster that makes shows for everyone. Sometimes, people at work might have disagreements, and the BBC has rules to handle complaints fairly. Kaye is speaking out to protect her reputation because she cares about her career and how people see her.
Article_Structure: WHO: Kaye Adams, a Scottish TV and radio presenter
WHAT: She denies false stories about her behavior after leaving BBC Scotland
WHERE: BBC Scotland and on social media like Instagram
WHY: To clear her name and show she worked hard for 15 years

perspectives.description: She says the stories are untrue and is sad about her reputation being hurt.
